justification: All pass criteria met: 9 failure modes identified (F1-F9), well exceeding the 5 minimum. Phantom charge risk explicitly identified in F5 with idempotency key mitigation. Fire-and-forget notification weakness addressed thoroughly with transactional outbox replacement. Circuit breaker and timeout patterns recommended with specific parameters (5 failures/30s, 5s/10s timeouts, bulkhead with 20 concurrent calls). Idempotency key for payment retries specified with concrete format (order_{order_id}_payment_{attempt}) and Stripe header forwarding. Trade-offs for added complexity acknowledged in detailed trade-offs table covering sync vs async, outbox vs CDC, idempotency vs 2PC, and circuit breaker vs queue decoupling.

observations.text: Identified 9 distinct failure modes including the subtle F9 thundering herd on recovery, which goes well beyond the expected 5
Phantom charge risk (F5) identified with clear explanation of the data inconsistency scenario and concrete mitigation via idempotency keys
Fire-and-forget replacement with transactional outbox pattern is well-designed, including dead letter store and FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED detail
Circuit breaker parameters are specific: 5 failures in 30s to open, 30s cooldown, 1 probe in half-open, plus bulkhead with 20 concurrent calls
Implementation guidance is phased and actionable with specific file paths, retry parameters, and concrete pitfalls to avoid
Distinguishes between transient failures worth retrying and permanent failures requiring circuit breaking
Explicitly warns against retrying without idempotency keys in pitfalls section
Saga/compensation pattern recommended for the partial failure case (payment succeeds, order persistence fails)

Retry backoff parameters in implementation guidance (base 1s, max 30s) differ from the expected rubric suggestion (100ms/500ms/1000ms) — though the chosen values are reasonable for external API calls to Stripe

agent_output_excerpt:
## Failure Mode Analysis & Resilience Architecture

### Executive Summary

This architecture has three critical failure vectors: synchronous coupling between Order→Payment creates cascading failures under load, the Payment→Stripe dependency introduces unbounded latency and single-point-of-failure risk, and the fire-and-forget notification pattern silently drops customer emails with no recovery path.
